queue.title: Refactor repo into monorepo skeleton while preserving existing website behavior
queue.notes: Verified. Existing website source bytes and local HTTP behavior were preserved.

queue.title: Import V1 data-lake code, SQL, docs, and tests into monorepo boundaries
queue.notes: Verified. V1 data-lake app, SQL, docs, and tests were imported into monorepo boundaries with monorepo path updates and credential-path hardening only.

queue.title: Implement batch runner with run ID, 45-minute DynamoDB TTL lock, status output, sanitized durable logs, and safe manual command
queue.notes: Verified. Local batch runner foundation writes run IDs, 45-minute TTL lock metadata, immutable status files, sanitized logs, and safe manual dry-run output. Execute mode deliberately fails until production extraction orchestration lands.

queue.title: Add production raw refresh orchestration using the existing GET-only extractor and full core-source refresh strategy
expectedEvidence: manual local orchestration run against controlled output paths
queue.notes: Verified. Execute-mode raw refresh orchestration is wired through the batch runner, locally verified with a fake GET-only client and controlled output paths. No live GHL extraction or AWS resource changes were run.

queue.title: Add idempotent recording archival skip logic keyed by stable GHL call message ID
expectedEvidence: bounded static review for no recording URL exposure
queue.notes: Verified. Recording archive keys are stable by message ID and existing archived recordings are skipped before download. No live recording downloads were run.

queue.title: Add run-safe curated publishing, latest-success freshness semantics, immutable run-status files, and lightweight stage-history snapshot/diff support
queue.notes: Verified. Curated output now uses snapshot_date/run_id partition semantics, rows carry snapshot_at, latest-success payload is produced only after writes, and opportunity_stage_history captures lightweight stage/status snapshots.

queue.title: Add container packaging, ECR image tagging, ECS Fargate task definition, IAM, public-subnet/no-NAT networking, and EventBridge Scheduler at 30-minute cadence
queue.notes: Completed after delayed Docker image build verification. Earlier local app/static/Terraform gates had passed; an isolated ARM64 Colima profile built apps/data-lake/Dockerfile successfully as gold-coast-data-lake:efc79ff, image inspect returned linux/arm64, docker run --rm gold-coast-data-lake:efc79ff --help returned CLI usage, Terraform now pins the task runtime platform through task_cpu_architecture=ARM64 by default, and terraform fmt/init/validate passed by 2026-05-19T07:40:16-04:00. No AWS resources, deploy, schedule enablement, live GHL extraction, Slack webhook call, or GitHub push was run.

queue.title: Add AWS-native Slack webhook alerts to C0B4JTC5VPF
queue.notes: Verified by owner. Added sanitized Slack alert module, batch runner alert callback, Fargate env/secret wiring, docs, and tests. No webhook call, live GHL extraction, AWS change, deploy, schedule enablement, or GitHub push was run.

queue.title: Add Athena smoke checks, run-status Athena table, and evidence docs
queue.notes: Verified by owner. Added Athena run-status DDL, smoke SQL, operator docs, single-line historical status files, S3 run-status/log publishing for non-dry-run execute mode, top-level image_tag/cloudwatch_log_url observability fields, and dry-run status-upload protection. No live Athena, GHL extraction, AWS change, deploy, schedule enablement, Slack webhook call, or GitHub push was run.

queue.title: Enable approved schedule and verify the first scheduled production run
queue.notes: Started Slice 10 preflight. Read-only AWS checks confirmed the data-lake bucket, Glue database, Athena workgroup, default VPC, and public subnets are available. Created missing Secrets Manager secret goldcoast/ghl-location-id from the approved local GHL credential source without exposing the value. Blocked before Terraform apply, image push, manual run, schedule enablement, or live GHL extraction because no Slack incoming-webhook secret for C0B4JTC5VPF was found; launching the production schedule without the approved AWS-owned alert path would violate the epic alert contract.

queue.title: Final acceptance and evidence bundle
queue.notes: Started earlier and remains blocked on unmet dependency: Slice 10 schedule enablement and first production run must complete first. The Slice 7 Docker build and runtime-platform gate cleared on 2026-05-19T07:40:16-04:00, so Slice 10 is now next. Do not perform final acceptance or mark the goal complete until Slice 10 completes and the final report is sent and recorded.

childRuns.notes: Worker returned completion. Owner verified local artifacts directly and accepted after the narrow verification gate passed.
Worker artifacts were consolidated and owner-hardened. Owner verification passed and Slice 9 was accepted.

lastVerifiedEvidence: AWS Secrets Manager was checked by name only; no Slack/webhook secrets were listed, and expected tech-alerts webhook secret names were missing.
Required local launch artifacts remain present: data-lake Dockerfile/package, refresh Terraform, runtime docs, run-status smoke docs, run-status DDL, and Slice 10 evidence.
Focused guardrail scans found no data-lake GHL mutating methods and no committed Slack webhook/token or high-risk secret patterns in scoped roots.

Decision: keep Slice 10 blocked before Terraform apply, image push, manual ECS run, EventBridge schedule enablement, first production GHL extraction, or Slack webhook call until the approved Slack webhook secret exists.
No live GHL extraction, AWS modification, Terraform plan/apply, ECR image push, ECS run, schedule enablement, Slack webhook call, routine Slack message, or GitHub push was run.

blockers.notes: Production launch requires the approved AWS-owned Slack webhook path for C0B4JTC5VPF. Rechecked 2026-05-19T08:03:52-04:00 by AWS Secrets Manager name-only list/describe; no Slack/webhook secret was found. Need the webhook URL stored in Secrets Manager, for example as goldcoast/slack/tech-alerts-webhook, before deploying/enabling the schedule with alerts.

releaseBlockers.notes: Do not enable the 30-minute production schedule or call the Slack alert path until the approved C0B4JTC5VPF webhook secret exists in AWS Secrets Manager. Rechecked 2026-05-19T08:03:52-04:00; expected tech-alerts webhook secret names are still missing.

guardrails: No local/OpenClaw cron dependency in deployed operations

resourceManifest.name: goldcoast/ghl-location-id
resourceManifest.notes: Contains the GHL location ID value from the approved local credential source. Secret value was not logged or committed.
